title: Madrid 3-Day City Break
metaTitle: Madrid 3-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Madrid, Spain: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Museo del Prado and Museo Reina Sofía. Prices from $982 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Madrid, Spain — Continental Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers (highs around 32-33°C in July) and cool winters (highs around 10°C in January), among Europe's sunniest capitals. April to June and September to October for mild weather; the July-August heat is intense. This itinerary blends Museo del Prado, Museo Reina Sofía, Plaza Mayor with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Museo del Prado, Museo Reina Sofía, Plaza Mayor
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Madrid
itinerary.description: Land at Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport (MAD) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Museo del Prado — World-renowned collection of European masters including Velázquez and Goya. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Madrid: Museo Reina Sofía & Royal Palace of Madrid
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Museo Reina Sofía — Modern art museum, home to Picasso's Guernica. In the afternoon, continue to Royal Palace of Madrid — Europe's largest functioning royal palace by floor area. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Museo Thyssen-Bornemisza or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to MAD for your departure flight.

travelTips: The Prado, Reina Sofía and Thyssen-Bornemisza offer free entry during specific evening hours.
Restaurants often don't open for dinner before 8:30-9pm.
Book DiverXO or other top tables months ahead.
Sightsee early morning or evening in July-August to avoid the worst heat.
El Rastro flea market on Sunday mornings is a Madrid institution worth planning around.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Lightweight clothing for hot summer days
A light jacket for cool evenings and winter
A smarter outfit for nicer restaurants and flamenco tablaos
thingsToDo: Cycling Madrid Río park along the Manzanares
Hot air ballooning over the Castilian countryside
thingsToAvoid: Overpaying for tapas at tourist-focused bars directly on Plaza Mayor.
Pickpockets on crowded Metro lines and around Puerta del Sol.
Visiting major museums on Mondays, when some are closed.
Underestimating how hot and exposed Retiro Park and outdoor plazas get in summer.
topRestaurants: Mercado de San Miguel — Spanish Tapas & Street Food ($$)
Taberna La Bola — Traditional Cocido Madrileño ($$)
hiddenGems: Palacio de Cristal in Retiro Park
San Antonio de la Florida chapel (Goya frescoes)
adventureActivities: Cycling Madrid Río park along the Manzanares
Hot air ballooning over the Castilian countryside
Hiking in the nearby Sierra de Guadarrama
luxuryExperiences: Private Prado Museum guided tour before opening
Suite stay at Hotel Ritz Madrid
Private flamenco show at an exclusive tablao
budgetExperiences: Free entry to the Prado during select evening hours
Wandering Plaza Mayor and Puerta del Sol

festivalCalendar.name: Madrid Pride
festivalCalendar.timing: Late June to early July, one of the largest Pride celebrations in Europe

localEtiquette: Dinner is eaten late (9-11pm); many restaurant kitchens don't open before 8:30pm.
Some shops still close for a couple of hours in the early afternoon.
Greet acquaintances with two cheek kisses.
Tipping isn't obligatory; 5-10% for good service is generous.
Madrileños socialize very late — nightlife often doesn't peak until after midnight.
localTransportGuide: Madrid's Metro is one of the most extensive underground systems in Europe, fast, cheap and covering nearly the whole city with frequent trains. The Multi ticket card works across metro, bus and local trains, and taxis are plentiful and metered fairly compared to many capitals.
bestTimeToVisit: April to June and September to October for mild weather; the July-August heat is intense.
weather: Continental Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers (highs around 32-33°C in July) and cool winters (highs around 10°C in January), among Europe's sunniest capitals.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a Schengen visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~10.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Madrid?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a Schengen visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Madrid?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to October for mild weather; the July-August heat is intense. Continental Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers (highs around 32-33°C in July) and cool winters (highs around 10°C in January), among Europe's sunniest capitals.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Madrid safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Madrid is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Madrid?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Elena Brown
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Faunia nature and wildlife park and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Ava Dubois
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Plaza Mayor alone was worth the trip, but combined with Mercado de San Miguel this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Elena Sharma
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Museo Reina Sofía and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Emma Williams
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Our trip to Madrid was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Royal Palace of Madrid was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Garcia
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Faunia nature and wildlife park alone was worth the trip, but combined with Retiro Park this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Olivia Khan
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Our trip to Madrid was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Mirador Madrid (Palacio de Cibeles rooftop) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Ivanov
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Templo de Debod alone was worth the trip, but combined with Mirador Madrid (Palacio de Cibeles rooftop) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Nadia Nguyen
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Madrid is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Mercado de San Miguel. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Sarah Garcia
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Madrid is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Gran Vía. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Chen Cohen
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Museo del Prado in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.
